chemistry.scope_summary: Dimerization of methyl cinnamate to form truxinate dimers with improved conversion and diastereoselectivity using thiourea catalyst

figures.key_information: Photochemical reactor setup with laser irradiation of conical glass vessel
figures.caption: Figure 1. A) Continuous flow photochemistry platform with directional irradiation  by an external light source; B) photoreactor with cone-shaped heat exchanger  as stabilizing element for the capillary.   Adapted with permission from ref. [21]  copyright 2015 Wiley-VCH.  | Figure 2. Synthesis of methyl δ-truxinate  2  via [2+2] photocycloaddition of  methyl cinnamate  1  using thiourea organocatalyst  3 .  | Figure 3. Continuous flow synthesis of (fluoromethoxy)benzene  5  with carbon  nitride as solid photocatalyst in a triphasic slug flow system.

figures.flow_path: three separate inlets converge into microfluidic chip then flow through coiled tubular reactor
figures.key_information: Liquid: 500 μL min⁻¹, Gas: 300 μL min⁻¹, Suspension: 50 μL min⁻¹
figures.caption: Figure 4. A) Suspension dosing device for triphasic mixing (inset: schematic  representation of gas-liquid-solid contacting); B) Resulting slug flow with solid  photocatalyst in the liquid phase; C) FEP capillary reactor with SMBRs under  steady state conditions. Adapted with permission from ref. [24] copyright 2018  Wiley-VCH.  | Figure 5. A) Self-made three-layer FEP capillary photoreactor used for the  experimental determination of photon fluxes in separate capillaries; B)  schematic representation of the capillaries wrapped around the central Hg lamp.  Adapted with permission from ref. [13g] copyright 2018 Wiley-VCH.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: This is a review paper focusing on flow photoreactor designs rather than comparing specific batch vs flow reactions
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Flow photochemistry enables better light-matter interaction, improved heat management, enhanced process control, and precise residence time control compared to traditional batch methods
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design and light delivery system - transition from batch vessels to continuous flow reactors with various configurations
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Core photochemical reaction mechanisms and fundamental chemistry
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Better temperature control and heat management in flow reactors reduces safety risks

figure_descriptions: Multiple photoreactor configurations including capillary reactors, microfluidic devices, and specialized scale-up designs
Various light coupling strategies and reactor geometries
Advanced reactor concepts including LSC microreactors and 3D printed devices

conflicts: Pass 3 indicates no BPR present, but Figure 15 clearly shows BPR component in reactor scheme
notes: This is a comprehensive review paper on flow photoreactor designs rather than a specific reaction optimization study. Limited quantitative data available as expected for a review format. BPR presence corrected based on figure evidence.
